skill.name: ai-image-generation
skill.description: Generate and edit images on RunComfy via the `runcomfy` CLI — a smart router across the full image-model catalog: FLUX 2 (Klein 9B/4B, Pro, Dev, Flash, Turbo, Max), Google Nano Banana 2 / Pro, OpenAI GPT Image 2, ByteDance Seedream 5 / 4-5 / 4-0 and Dreamina 4-0, Alibaba Qwen Image and Z-Image Turbo, Wan 2-7. Covers both text-to-image (t2i) and image-to-image / edit (i2i) endpoints — the skill picks the right model for the user's actual intent (typography precision, photoreal portraits, sub-second iteration, multi-reference brand styling, open-weights workflow) and ships each model's documented prompting patterns plus the minimal `runcomfy run` invoke. Triggers on "generate image", "make a picture", "text to image", "AI image", "make an image of …", "image to image", "i2i", or any explicit ask to create or restyle an image.
skill.summary: Generate and edit images on RunComfy via the runcomfy CLI with 11+ AI models
skill.author: doany-ai

security_audit.summary: All static findings are false positives. The skill is legitimate AI image generation documentation that uses the runcomfy CLI. Backtick patterns are markdown code formatting, weak crypto flags are YAML syntax, and path traversal flags are placeholder URLs. The skill explicitly documents security considerations including shell injection prevention (prompts passed as JSON, not shell-expanded) and scope limiting (only runcomfy commands). No malicious intent detected.

low_findings.title: Backtick Syntax in Documentation
low_findings.description: Static analyzer detected backtick execution patterns throughout SKILL.md. These are markdown inline code formatting for documenting command syntax, not actual code execution. All backticks appear in documentation examples within code blocks.

low_findings.title: Placeholder URLs in Documentation
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged path traversal sequences at example URL locations. These are placeholder URL patterns like 'https://.../portrait.jpg' in documentation, not actual path traversal vulnerabilities.

dangerous_patterns.title: Inline Code Formatting (False Positive)
dangerous_patterns.description: Backtick syntax like `blackforestlabs/flux-2-klein/9b/text-to-image` throughout SKILL.md is markdown inline code formatting for documenting CLI syntax. Not Ruby/shell backtick execution.

dangerous_patterns.title: YAML Frontmatter Syntax (False Positive)
dangerous_patterns.description: The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' flag on line 5 refers to YAML frontmatter with `> ` folding syntax. Not related to cryptography.

content.user_title: Generate and edit images with 11+ AI models
content.value_statement: Creating images for marketing, design, or creative work requires choosing the right AI model. This skill routes your request to the optimal model for typography, photoreal portraits, speed, or brand styling and generates the exact CLI command.
actual_capabilities: Generate images with 11+ AI models including FLUX 2, GPT Image 2, Nano Banana, Seedream, and Qwen Image
Edit existing images with identity preservation using i2i (image-to-image) endpoints
Choose the optimal model based on intent: typography, photoreal portraits, speed, or brand styling
Handle multilingual image text with exact rendering for Japanese, Cyrillic, Arabic, and other scripts
Generate batch variants for moodboards and ideation with 4-up generation
Produce images up to 4K resolution for final delivery
limitations: Requires a RunComfy account and API token for authentication
Generated images depend on AI model capabilities and may require iteration
In-image text accuracy varies by model; GPT Image 2 handles typography best
Output quality and style consistency varies between different AI model families

use_cases.title: Marketing team creates brand campaign assets
use_cases.description: Generate multilingual campaign posters with exact headlines in multiple languages using GPT Image 2, then batch-create social media variants with Nano Banana for rapid iteration.

use_cases.title: Developer generates product imagery for documentation
use_cases.description: Create consistent product screenshots, UI mockups, or technical diagrams using Seedream for photoreal quality or FLUX for design-style illustrations.

use_cases.title: Creative director explores visual concepts rapidly
use_cases.description: Produce 10+ concept variations at speed using Nano Banana 2 with 0.5K resolution for early exploration, then upgrade to FLUX 2 Klein 9B for final polished deliverables.

prompt_templates.title: Basic text-to-image generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a photoreal image of [describe subject and scene]. Use a [style] aesthetic with [lighting description].

prompt_templates.title: Typography with exact text
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a [format] poster with the headline reading exactly "[exact headline text]" in [font style] on [background]. [Additional visual elements].
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate: Marketing asset with specific text requirements

prompt_templates.title: Brand-consistent image edit
prompt_templates.prompt: Edit this image to [describe changes]. Keep the [elements to preserve] unchanged. Apply [brand style or color palette] to match our brand guidelines. Image: [user provides image URL]
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced: Preserving brand identity while making targeted edits

prompt_templates.title: Batch concept exploration
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate 4 variations of [describe concept] at [resolution] resolution. Keep [consistent elements] the same across all variants but vary [elements to change].
prompt_templates.scenario: Power user: Rapid ideation with controlled variation

output_examples.input: Generate a photoreal portrait of a person by a window with soft natural light
output_examples.output: Produces a high-quality portrait image saved to ./out directory with proper lighting and depth of field

output_examples.input: Create a poster with the headline reading exactly "SPRING SALE" in bold white sans-serif on a navy background
output_examples.output: Produces a marketing poster with precise typography, exact text rendering, and proper layout

output_examples.input: Edit this product photo to change the background to a rainy cyberpunk street while keeping the product unchanged
output_examples.output: Produces an edited image with the product preserved and new cyberpunk background applied

best_practices: Lead with the subject in prompts: 'A cat on a stone...' rather than 'Generate an image of a cat on a stone'
Match resolution to use case: use 0.5K for rapid iteration and 2K+ only for final deliverables to manage costs
Preserve brand identity in edits by leading prompts with what to keep, ending with what to change
anti_patterns: Avoid vague creative instructions like 'make it pop' or 'add more style' - be specific about visual elements
Do not request arbitrary image sizes outside model-supported ratios (e.g., GPT Image 2 only supports 1:1, 2:3, 3:2)
Avoid chaining too many edits in a single prompt - iterate compound changes in separate passes for better results

faq.question: Which model should I use for text in images?
faq.answer: GPT Image 2 is best for typography. Quote the exact text you want rendered in your prompt like 'the headline reads exactly "SPRING SALE"'.

faq.question: How do I generate images quickly for exploration?
faq.answer: Use Nano Banana 2 with 0.5K resolution and num_images: 4 for batch ideation. FLUX 2 Klein 4B also offers sub-second generation.

faq.question: Which model produces the best photoreal portraits?
faq.answer: Seedream 5 Lite excels at photoreal skin tones and natural lighting. FLUX 2 Klein 9B is also strong for photoreal work with proper prompting.

faq.question: How do I edit an image while keeping certain elements unchanged?
faq.answer: Lead your prompt with preservation goals: 'Keep the person's face and clothing unchanged. Convert the background to...' Use FLUX Kontext Pro for precise single-image edits.

faq.question: Can I use reference images for style guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. FLUX 2 Klein supports up to 4 reference images for style transfer. Nano Banana 2 supports web search grounding with enable_web_search: true for real-world matching.

faq.question: What resolution should I use for final deliverables?
faq.answer: Use 2K or 4K resolution only for final delivery. 0.5K is sufficient for ideation and drafts. Higher resolution costs significantly more (4K is ~16x the cost of 0.5K).
